learningObjectives: Use communication and media vocabulary at A2 level.
Report simple statements about media habits.
Use zero and first conditionals to talk about messages and online habits.
Find detail and opinion in a short survey text.
Understand main ideas in a radio interview and safety announcement.
Write a short message about media use.
Answer interview questions about online communication.
unit.readingSkillFocus: Multiple Choice Long Text (Detail and Opinion)

vocabularyBank.exampleSentence: I sent a message to my friend after school.
The food blogger posted a new recipe today.
She subscribed to a science channel on the internet.
An advertisement appeared before the video started.
The headline said 'School Team Wins Prize'.
Tom subscribed to three gaming channels.
I got a notification about a new message.
Keep your profile private on social media.
We listen to a podcast about space in class.
The channel uploads new content every Friday.
Do not upload photos without permission.
The video has ten thousand viewers this week.

grammarReference.explanation: To report what someone said, often use said/told + (that) + subject + past tense. Pronouns and time words may change.
examples: Direct: "I subscribe to this channel." → Reported: She said (that) she subscribed to that channel.
Direct: "I read the headline." → Reported: He said (that) he read the headline.
Direct: "My profile is private." → Reported: She told us (that) her profile was private.
commonMistakes: She said me she likes blogs (×) → She told me she liked blogs (✓)
He said that he subscribe (×) → He said that he subscribed (✓)
They told they upload photos (×) → They said they uploaded photos (✓)

grammarReference.explanation: Zero conditional: If + present, present — for general truths. First conditional: If + present, will + base verb — for real future possibilities.
examples: If you subscribe, you get notifications. (zero)
If I see a strange message, I tell my parents. (zero)
If the advertisement is boring, I will skip it. (first)
If Mia writes a positive headline, more viewers will read her blog. (first)
commonMistakes: If I will see a message, I tell (×) → If I see a message, I will tell (✓)
If you subscribe, you will get always (×) → If you subscribe, you always get notifications (✓)
If he uploads, he will uploads (×) → If he uploads, he will get viewers (✓)

lessons.title: Lesson 1: Communication and Media Words
lessons.learningObjective: Recognise and understand twelve words about messages, blogs and online media at KET A2 level.

exercises.title: Learn: Media Word Match
exercises.instructions: Read each sentence. Choose the best word.

questions.questionText: A short text you send on your phone is a _____.
questions.explanation: Message (tin nhắn) gửi qua điện thoại. Headline là tiêu đề tin.

questions.questionText: A person who writes regular posts online is a _____.
questions.explanation: Blogger (người viết blog) đăng bài thường xuyên. Viewer là người xem.

questions.questionText: When you follow a video series online, you join a _____.
questions.explanation: Channel (kênh) là nơi đăng video. Podcast là chương trình audio.

questions.questionText: A paid promotion before a video is an _____.
questions.explanation: Advertisement (quảng cáo) xuất hiện trước nội dung. Content là nội dung chính.

questions.questionText: The big title at the top of news is the _____.
questions.explanation: Headline (tiêu đề tin) ở đầu bài báo. Profile là trang cá nhân.

questions.questionText: When you press 'follow' on a channel, you _____.
questions.explanation: Subscribe (đăng ký theo dõi) để nhận nội dung mới.

exercises.title: Practice: Match the Pairs
exercises.instructions: Match each word on the left with the correct meaning on the right.

questions.questionText: Match the media words to their meanings.
questions.explanation: Notification và profile thường bị nhầm — một cái là báo, một cái là trang cá nhân.

pairs.right: an alert on your phone or app
to put photos or videos on the internet
a person who watches online content

exercises.title: Check: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: A weekly audio show you download is a _____.
questions.explanation: Podcast là chương trình audio. Channel thường là video.

questions.questionText: Videos, photos and posts together are called online _____.
questions.explanation: Content (nội dung) gồm mọi thứ đăng tải.

questions.questionText: Be careful — do not _____ personal photos without asking.
questions.explanation: Upload (tải lên) cần cẩn thận với quyền riêng tư.

questions.questionText: The blogger has five thousand _____ this month.
questions.explanation: Viewers (người xem) xem nội dung. Notifications là thông báo.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Sentences
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct word from the unit.

questions.questionText: Complete: I got a [0] about a new [1] from my cousin.
questions.explanation: notification (thông báo) về message (tin nhắn).

content.template: I got a [0] about a new [1] from my cousin.

questions.questionText: Complete: She [0] to a cooking [1] and reads every [2].
questions.explanation: subscribed; channel; headline — thói quen xem media.

content.template: She [0] to a cooking [1] and reads every [2].

exercises.title: Review: Vocab Quiz
exercises.instructions: No hints — choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: A weekly audio show you download is a _____.
questions.explanation: Podcast là chương trình audio. Channel thường là video.

questions.questionText: Videos, photos and posts together are called online _____.
questions.explanation: Content (nội dung) gồm mọi thứ đăng tải.

questions.questionText: Be careful — do not _____ personal photos without asking.
questions.explanation: Upload (tải lên) cần cẩn thận với quyền riêng tư.

questions.questionText: The blogger has five thousand _____ this month.
questions.explanation: Viewers (người xem) xem nội dung. Notifications là thông báo.

lessons.title: Lesson 2: Reported Speech and Conditionals
lessons.learningObjective: Report simple statements about media and use zero and first conditionals correctly.

exercises.title: Learn: Report What They Said
exercises.instructions: Choose the best reported sentence.

questions.questionText: Direct: "I subscribe to this channel." → Reported:
questions.explanation: She said (that) she subscribed — động từ lùi thì trong reported speech.

choices.text: She said (that) she subscribe to this channel.
She said (that) she subscribed to that channel.

choices.distractorNote: said me is wrong — told me

questions.questionText: Direct: "My profile is private." → Reported:
questions.explanation: She told us (that) her profile was private — is → was.

choices.text: She told us her profile is private.
She told us (that) her profile was private.
She told that profile was private.

questions.questionText: If you subscribe, you _____ notifications. (general truth)
questions.explanation: Zero conditional: If + present, present.

questions.questionText: If the advertisement is boring, I _____ it. (future result)
questions.explanation: First conditional: If + present, will + verb.

exercises.title: Practice: Complete the Grammar
exercises.instructions: Fill in each gap with the correct form.

questions.questionText: Complete: Tom said he [0] to a gaming channel. | If I [1] a strange message, I [2] my parents.
questions.explanation: subscribed (reported); get / see; tell — zero conditional.

content.template: Tom said he [0] to a gaming channel. If I [1] a strange message, I [2] my parents.

questions.questionText: Complete: If she [0] a good headline, more viewers [1] her blog.
questions.explanation: writes (first conditional if-clause); will read.

content.template: If she [0] a good headline, more viewers [1] her blog.

exercises.title: Check: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Mia said, "I upload content every Friday." → Reported:
questions.explanation: Mia said (that) she uploaded content every Friday.

choices.text: Mia said (that) she uploads content every Friday.
Mia said (that) she uploaded content every Friday.
Mia told she uploaded content every Friday.

questions.questionText: If you _____ your profile public, strangers can see your photos.
questions.explanation: Zero conditional: If + present, can + verb.

questions.questionText: If I read an interesting headline, I _____ the full message.
questions.explanation: First conditional for real future: I will read.

questions.questionText: Priya told us she _____ photos only on weekends.
questions.explanation: Reported: upload → uploaded.

exercises.title: Apply: Build the Sentence
exercises.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to make a sentence.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: He / said / that / he / subscribed / to the channel.
questions.explanation: He said that he subscribed to the channel. — reported speech.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: If / I / get / a notification, / I / check / the message.
questions.explanation: If I get a notification, I check the message. — zero conditional.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: If / the advertisement / is boring, / I / will / skip / it.
questions.explanation: If the advertisement is boring, I will skip it. — first conditional.

exercises.title: Review: Grammar Challenge
exercises.instructions: Choose the correct answer. Think carefully.

questions.questionText: Mia said, "I upload content every Friday." → Reported:
questions.explanation: Mia said (that) she uploaded content every Friday.

choices.text: Mia said (that) she uploads content every Friday.
Mia said (that) she uploaded content every Friday.
Mia told she uploaded content every Friday.

questions.questionText: If you _____ your profile public, strangers can see your photos.
questions.explanation: Zero conditional: If + present, can + verb.

questions.questionText: If I read an interesting headline, I _____ the full message.
questions.explanation: First conditional for real future: I will read.

questions.questionText: Priya told us she _____ photos only on weekends.
questions.explanation: Reported: upload → uploaded.

lessons.title: Lesson 3: Media Survey
lessons.learningObjective: Find detail, opinion and vocabulary in a short survey about communication and media habits.

exercises.title: Learn: Read the Survey
exercises.instructions: Read the survey results. Answer the detail questions.

passage.title: Teen Media Habits Survey
passage.text:
WEST PARK SCHOOL — MEDIA SURVEY RESULTS

Last month, 120 students answered questions about communication and media. Here are three opinions from the report.

Student A — Linh, age 13
Linh checks her phone for notifications every hour. She follows one food blogger and two video channels about science. She said, "If I see an interesting headline, I read the full message straight away."

Student B — Tom, age 14
Tom prefers short messages to long emails. He subscribed to a gaming channel last year and sometimes watches live content after homework. He explained, "If the advertisement is boring, I skip it."

Student C — Priya, age 13
Priya is careful about her online profile. She uploads photos only on weekends and never shares personal details in a message to strangers. She told us, "If someone sends a strange notification, I tell my parents."

The school will use these answers to plan safer media lessons next term.
passage.imagePrompt: School survey infographic with three student profiles, phone icons, video play buttons and notification badges; friendly teen magazine style.

questions.questionText: How often does Linh check notifications?
questions.explanation: Linh checks her phone for notifications every hour.

questions.questionText: What does Tom do if an advertisement is boring?
questions.explanation: Tom: "If the advertisement is boring, I skip it."

exercises.title: Practice: Words in the Survey
exercises.instructions: Read the survey again. Answer about vocabulary and meaning.

passage.title: Teen Media Habits Survey
passage.text:

Student B — Tom, age 14

Student C — Priya, age 13

The school will use these answers to plan safer media lessons next term.
passage.imagePrompt: School survey infographic with three student profiles, phone icons, video play buttons and notification badges; friendly teen magazine style.

questions.questionText: What does 'subscribed' mean in Tom's answer?
questions.explanation: He subscribed to a gaming channel — đăng ký theo dõi kênh.

choices.text: He followed the channel to get new videos

questions.questionText: Why is Priya careful about her profile?
questions.explanation: She never shares personal details — safety and privacy.

choices.text: She wants to stay safe online

exercises.title: Check: Main Idea and Order
exercises.instructions: Read the survey one more time. These questions need careful thinking.

lessons.title: Lesson 4: Media on the Radio
lessons.learningObjective: Understand detail in a radio interview about a blogger and an online safety announcement.

exercises.title: Learn: Blogger Interview
exercises.instructions: Listen to the radio item. Answer the first two questions.

script.title: School Radio Interview with a Blogger

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Today on West Park Radio, we talk to teen blogger Mia Chen. Mia, thanks for coming.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Mia said she writes a short message to her readers every morning. Her channel has five thousand subscribers now.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: She told us, if a headline is negative, she tries to write something positive instead.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Mia also said students should check notifications carefully and keep their profile private. Back to music after this advertisement.

script.audioNotes: Upbeat student radio tone, one speaker, clear diction. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: How many subscribers does Mia's channel have?
questions.explanation: Her channel has five thousand subscribers now.

questions.questionText: What does Mia try to write if a headline is negative?
questions.explanation: If a headline is negative, she tries to write something positive.

exercises.title: Practice: More from the Interview
exercises.instructions: Listen again to the same radio item. Answer the next questions.

script.title: School Radio Interview with a Blogger

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Today on West Park Radio, we talk to teen blogger Mia Chen. Mia, thanks for coming.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Mia said she writes a short message to her readers every morning. Her channel has five thousand subscribers now.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: She told us, if a headline is negative, she tries to write something positive instead.

lines.speaker: Host
lines.text: Mia also said students should check notifications carefully and keep their profile private. Back to music after this advertisement.

script.audioNotes: Upbeat student radio tone, one speaker, clear diction. Approx. 45 seconds.

questions.questionText: What does Mia write to her readers every morning?
questions.explanation: She writes a short message to her readers every morning.

questions.questionText: What does Mia say students should do with their profile?
questions.explanation: Keep their profile private.

exercises.title: Check: Online Safety Announcement
exercises.instructions: Listen to a new announcement. Choose the correct answer.

script.title: Online Safety Announcement

lines.speaker: Ms Ortiz
lines.text: Good afternoon. This is a reminder about online communication and media safety.

lines.speaker: Ms Ortiz
lines.text: If you get a message from someone you do not know, do not reply. Tell a teacher or your parents.

lines.speaker: Ms Ortiz
lines.text: Remember: if you subscribe to a channel, check the profile carefully before you upload photos.

lines.speaker: Ms Ortiz
lines.text: If an advertisement asks for personal details, ignore it. Thank you.

script.audioNotes: Clear PA voice, serious but friendly, moderate pace. Approx. 40 seconds.

questions.questionText: What should you do if you get a message from someone you do not know?
questions.explanation: Do not reply. Tell a teacher or your parents.

choices.text: Tell a teacher or your parents

questions.questionText: Before you upload photos, what should you check?
questions.explanation: Check the profile carefully before you upload photos.

exercises.title: Apply: Match Facts from the Interview
exercises.instructions: Listen to the blogger interview again. Match each topic to the correct fact.

lessons.title: Lesson 5: Write About Media
lessons.learningObjective: Write a short message about media habits using reported speech and conditionals.

exercises.title: Learn: Message Phrases
exercises.instructions: Complete each gap with a word from the box: message, channel, subscribe, if, will.

questions.questionText: Complete: I sent a [0] to my friend about a new video [1]. | [2] you [3] to it, you get notifications.
questions.explanation: message; channel; If; subscribe — zero conditional.

content.template: I sent a [0] to my friend about a new video [1]. [2] you [3] to it, you get notifications.

exercises.title: Practice: Build Sentences
exercises.instructions: Put the words in order to make correct sentences.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: She / said / that / she / read / the headline.
questions.explanation: She said that she read the headline. — reported speech in writing.

questions.questionText: Make a sentence: If / I / see / a boring advertisement, / I / will / skip / it.
questions.explanation: If I see a boring advertisement, I will skip it. — first conditional.

exercises.title: Check: Message About Your Media Habits
exercises.instructions: Write a message to a friend. Write at least 25 words.

content.taskDescription: Write a message to your friend about your communication and media habits. Mention a channel, blog or app you use.
prompts: Say which channel, blogger or app you use.
Explain when you check messages or notifications.
Use If ... to say what you do when you see an advertisement or headline.
End the message in a friendly way.

modelAnswer.text:
Hi Lan,

I subscribed to a science channel last month. I check notifications after school, but not during lessons. My friend said that she uploads photos on weekends only. If an advertisement is boring, I will skip it. Do you read the headline before you watch a video?

See you,
Tom

grammar.criteria: Uses reported speech or zero/first conditional correctly.

vocabulary.criteria: Uses media words (message, channel, subscribe, notification, etc.).

organization.criteria: Message is clear with greeting and closing.

taskAchievement.criteria: Describes media habits; at least 25 words.

questions.questionText: Write your response following the task.
questions.explanation: Chấm theo rubric. Bài mẫu cho thấy độ dài và nội dung mong đợi ở A2.

exercises.title: Apply: Complete the Message Frames
exercises.instructions: Complete the message frames with the correct unit words.

questions.questionText: Complete: Hi Mia, | I read your [0] today. | If I like the [1], I [2] to your [3]. | Bye, | Hoa
questions.explanation: headline; content; subscribe; channel.

content.template:
Hi Mia,

I read your [0] today. If I like the [1], I [2] to your [3].

Bye,
Hoa

exercises.title: Review: Message About Your Media Habits
exercises.instructions: Write a message to a friend. Write at least 25 words.

prompts: Say which channel, blogger or app you use.

lessons.title: Lesson 6: Talk About Media Habits
lessons.learningObjective: Answer interview questions about messages, channels and online safety.

exercises.title: Learn: Choose the Best Reply
exercises.instructions: Imagine an examiner asks you a question. Choose the best answer.

questions.questionText: Examiner: Do you subscribe to any channels? You say:
questions.explanation: Yes, I subscribe to... — trả lời trực tiếp với từ vựng đơn vị.

choices.text: Yes, I subscribe to a cooking channel and a music channel.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What is a notification? You say:
questions.explanation: An alert on your phone — định nghĩa ngắn A2.

choices.text: It is an alert on my phone about a new message or video.

exercises.title: Practice: Best Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the most appropriate reply in each media situation.

questions.questionText: Your friend said, 'I upload photos every day.' You report:
questions.explanation: She said (that) she uploaded... — reported speech trong nói.

choices.text: She said she upload photos every day.
She said that she uploaded photos every day.

questions.questionText: Examiner: What do you do if you see a strange message? You say:
questions.explanation: If I get a strange message, I tell my parents — zero conditional.

choices.text: If I will get strange message, tell.
If I get a strange message, I tell my parents.

exercises.title: Check: Interview About Media
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions. Record your answers.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.

exercises.title: Apply: Situational Response
exercises.instructions: Choose the best thing to say in each situation.

questions.questionText: A stranger asks for your profile link in a message. You say:
questions.explanation: Refuse politely and tell an adult — an toàn online.

choices.text: Here is my profile! Message me!
Sorry, I do not share my profile with strangers. I will tell my teacher.

questions.questionText: Your cousin asks why you subscribed to a channel. You say:
questions.explanation: Explain reason with because/if — A2 speaking pattern.

choices.text: Because the content is interesting and I learn new words from the blogger.

exercises.title: Review: Interview About Media
exercises.instructions: Answer the examiner's questions. Record your answers.

questions.questionText: Record your answers to the follow-up questions.
questions.explanation: Chấm tổng thể theo bốn tiêu chí. Trả lời ngắn vẫn được điểm một phần nếu dùng đúng từ vựng.
